notes: Pure onboarding. One full-strength player cell versus one weak red cell to teach the first direct connection.
Early branching pressure. One weak red falls first, then the player pivots into the second target.
First slice read with a strong player start, two cheap neutrals, and one distant red anchor.
Routing via two neutrals before the final push. This is no longer tagged as the first support-loop lesson.
First obstacle-routing phase. Two circle blockers force relay expansion before the red stronghold.
First mirrored route decision after the blocker intro. Two player starts can take opposite neutrals before collapsing the center.
Twin-front pressure. Neutral timing matters because the two reds punish slow expansion.
Asymmetric pressure. One supporting allied flank plus two contested neutrals before the stronger lower-right red.
First strong central defender. Player should avoid tunnel vision on the center until the side reds are stabilized.
Three-red pressure spread. The center neutral helps the player consolidate before the final outer push.
Wide expansion check. Three neutrals create safer pacing if claimed in order instead of rushed straight into the reds.
Central stronghold with side escorts. Encourages side trimming before the final breach.
Obstacle wave starts. Dual circles force clearer lane routing without turning the phase into a maze.
Obstacle-assisted center contest. The middle neutral becomes the safest pivot once the side blockers are read correctly.
Reinforced route-reading. Triple blockers force commitment choice instead of straight-line feeding.
Most obstacle-dense World 1 non-boss phase. Low power limit is the constraint: the player must disconnect and reconnect to advance.
Repeated-cut mastery check. Two mirrored red chains reward precise disruption and timing.
Support-loop mastery plus one strong finisher. Player opening is wider, but the late push must still be coordinated.
Pre-boss endurance. Three player starts can snowball only if neutrals are converted into a stable support web.
World 1 boss closure. Multi-stage red line with obstacle choke and late stronghold; meant to reward loop setup before the breach.
